---
paths:
  - "docker/**/*"
  - "docker/Dockerfile"
  - "docker/*.yml"
  - "docker/*.sh"
---
# Docker rules (ai-local-agent-tools)

## .env is generated by Ansible — NEVER edit manually
- docker/.env is written by hp1-infra repo (roles/hp1_deploy, env.j2 template)
- Editing it directly will be overwritten on next `ansible-playbook hp1_site.yml`
- To change a value: update the vault or group_vars in hp1-infra, re-run playbook

## docker stack deploy ALWAYS needs env sourced first
```bash
cd docker && set -a; source .env; set +a
docker stack deploy -c swarm-stack.yml hp1
```
`docker stack deploy` does NOT read .env files automatically — this is a known gotcha.

## Volumes (never change names — data lives here)
- agent-data (SQLite DB, skill exports)
- agent-logs
- agent-checkpoints
- agent-skills (hot-loaded skill modules)

## Build pattern
```bash
docker build \
  --build-arg DOCKER_GID=$(stat -c '%g' /var/run/docker.sock) \
  -t hp1-ai-agent:latest \
  -f docker/Dockerfile .
```
DOCKER_GID build arg needed for socket access in container.

## Port 8000 only in production
- 5173 is Vite dev server — never expose in swarm-stack.yml or compose
- GUI is served by FastAPI's StaticFiles mount at port 8000
- gui/dist is built by the Node.js stage in Dockerfile — not in repo

## Multi-stage Dockerfile stages
1. gui-builder: Node 20, npm ci && npm run build → gui/dist
2. builder: Python 3.13, install wheels
3. runtime: Python 3.13-slim, non-root `agent` user, copy wheels + dist + app

## Swarm stack (upgrade testing only)
- Stack name: hp1
- Swarm cluster: 3 managers (199.21-23) + 3 workers (199.31-33)
- The agent itself does NOT run in swarm — it runs standalone on 199.10
- Swarm is for testing Kafka, Elasticsearch, Filebeat, and other service upgrades

## Health check
```bash
curl -sf http://localhost:8000/api/health
# Should return {"status": "ok", ...}
```

## Agent-01 is the management VM — NOT a swarm service
- The agent, PostgreSQL, MuninnDB all run on agent-01 (199.10) as standalone containers
- The swarm cluster (managers/workers) runs Kafka, Elasticsearch, Logstash, Filebeat etc.
- SQLite and PostgreSQL live on agent-01 only — never in swarm, no concurrent write risk
- `API_WORKERS=1` — keep at 1 (uvicorn + SQLite single-writer requirement)
- If swarm-stack.yml ever deploys the agent: `replicas: 1` and PostgreSQL must be external first

## Filebeat stale alert threshold
- `ELASTIC_FILEBEAT_STALE_MINUTES=10` (default) drives the memory alert flood
- To tune: set in docker/.env, redeploy
- Root cause: Filebeat container on workers not shipping to Elasticsearch

## MuninnDB connection
- `MUNINNDB_URL=http://muninndb:9475` — must be set for agent memory to function
- MuninnDB container runs alongside agent on agent-01
- Required for pre_upgrade_check step 5 (past upgrade history lookup)
